event: developed after a minor trauma in 1967
started to grow over the past few years
would periodically drain, scab over and drain again
a portion of the mass had fallen out and it bled more profusely than it had before
developed severe diffuse swelling in her arms, legs and trunk
oxygen saturation of 100% on 4-l nasal cannula
severe upper and lower extremity pitting oedema
soft to touch with thick oozing purulent drainage
B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) level of 666 pg/ml
mild interstitial oedema on chest X-ray
attributed to recurrent bleeding from the neck mass
liver metastases from a mass suspicious for malignancy
transfused three units of packed red blood cells
ascribed the fever to a transfusion reaction
white blood cell count of 24.3*103/μl with 6% band forms
blood cultures eventually grew E. coli
creatinine had doubled from 0.72 mg/dl to 1.46 mg/dL
placed on bilevel non-invasive positive pressure ventilation
transferred to the medical intensive care unit (MICU)
urinalysis drawn upon arrival was notable for pyuria with greater than 180 white blood cells per hpf
obstructing left distal ureteric stone measuring up to 1.6 cm with proximal dilation and hydronephrosis
taken to the operating room for extraction and stenting
could not be extubated for a day
chest X-ray showed increasing pulmonary oedema
became oliguric with a creatinine elevated to 6.4 mg/dl
transferred back to the general medicine service
discharged from the hospital on day 28
required haemodialysis for several months before her kidney function recovered
pathology showed a benign haemangioma with infected haematoma and abscess formation
